summary: PR 2201 marked melee/it/items/itheart.c as Matching in configure.py and adjusted several GALE01 symbol metadata entries from it_* globals to local @ labels for data, sdata, and sdata2 addresses. The bot report recorded linked code and linked data increases. No PR body, review comments, or code-source changes were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Changed itheart.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) in MatchingFor.
Changed seven symbol entries at .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 addresses from it_* names to local @ labels, with some scope changes from global to local.

what_changed.text: melee/it/items/itheart.c was marked as Matching in configure.py.
One .data string at 0x803F5718 changed from global it_803F5718 to local @234.
Two .sdata strings at 0x804D5210 and 0x804D5218 changed from global it_* names to local @188 and @189.
Two local .sdata2 floats at 0x804DC8A8 and 0x804DC8AC changed from it_* names to local @156 and @157.
Two .sdata2 floats at 0x804DD970 and 0x804DD974 changed from global it_* names to local @162 and @163.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 linked code +0.04% (+1744 bytes) and linked data +0.01% (+144 bytes).

smart_moves.text: The matching-status change was paired with symbol metadata cleanup instead of source-code churn in the loaded diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: only configure.py and config/GALE01/symbols.txt changed

decomp_lessons.text: For a matching-status PR, symbol metadata can be part of the required closure: this slice adjusted local/global scope and local label names in symbols.txt before marking itheart.c as Matching.

naming_conventions.text: Local unnamed data labels in symbols.txt used @number form, such as @234, @188, @189, @156, @157, @162, and @163, replacing address-style it_* names where the PR marked entries local.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used configuration and symbol-table metadata changes, rather than C source edits in the loaded diff, to mark the itheart object matching.
Adjusted symbol scope/name metadata across .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 entries, consistent with data-section metadata being part of matching work.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number, title, author, merged_at, changed files
raw_diff: complete diff, 4388 chars, not truncated
text_corpus.jsonl: empty PR body and one bot report
